channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Patriots to introduce Mike Vrabel as new head coach at noon
item.description: The New England Patriots are set to formally introduce Mike Vrabel as the team's 16th head coach in franchise history.
item.dc:creator: Melanie DaSilva, NewsNation

item.title: Millions have had student loans canceled under Biden
item.description: Despite failing to deliver his promise for broad student loan forgiveness, President Joe Biden has now overseen the cancellation of student loans for more than 5 million Americans — more than any other president in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Newsom concerned Trump will try to withhold California fire aid
item.description: “He’s done it in Utah. He’s done it in Michigan, did it in Puerto Rico. He did it to California back before I was even governor, in 2018, until he found out folks in Orange County voted for him and he decided to give them money.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's inauguration weekend features fireworks, MAGA rally, inaugural balls
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration weekend will start with a fireworks display outside his northern Virginia golf club and end with a trio of inaugural balls where he will speak, the committee planning the events announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Living longer can mean more dementia but there are ways to reduce the risk
item.description: New research suggests that Americans' risk for developing dementia over a lifetime may be higher than previously thought.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russian forces bypass a key stronghold in a bid to cut off its supplies, a Ukrainian officer says
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian forces are bypassing a key stronghold in eastern Ukraine that they have fought for months to capture and are focusing instead on cutting supply lines to it, a Ukrainian official said Monday.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and YEHOR KONOVALOV, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: LA area wildfires: At least 24 have died, strong winds expected
item.description: Crews got some relief over the weekend as the winds died down, but the gusts are expected to pick up later.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: Crews gear up to battle a ruthless new threat in California wildfires and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hurts tosses 2 TD passes and Goedert throws 3 stiff-arms to lead Eagles past Packers 22-10
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Dallas Goedert fired one stiff arm to a Packers defender, smacked him again and then — in a sports city known for its Broad Street Bullies — dispatched one final shot to the face on his way to end zone.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Incarcerated firefighters join California wildfire battle efforts
item.description: The California Department of Corrections and Rehabilitation has deployed 931 incarcerated firefighters and 114 support staff through its Fire Camp Program.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Fencing, roof of Anchorage pedestrian bridge fall in strong wind, closing highway
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — The side fencing and roof of a pedestrian bridge fell in strong wind early Sunday, closing the main highway south of Anchorage in both directions until the debris could be cleared.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Healthcare system present in CA files for bankruptcy protection
item.description: The California-based company has hospitals in California, Connecticut, Pennsylvania, and Rhode Island.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: Massive 1,400-pound great white shark pings off Florida beach 4 times in 1 day
item.description: Shark researchers recently reported a 1,400-pound, 13-foot great white shark pinged off a Florida beach four times in one day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance says those who ‘committed violence’ on Jan. 6 ‘obviously’ shouldn’t be pardoned
item.description: (The Hill) -- Vice President-elect JD Vance said people who “committed violence” on Jan. 6 “obviously” shouldn’t be pardoned by President-elect Trump. “I think it’s very simple.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: A dad and daughter help evacuees learn whether their homes survived the California wildfires
item.description: A father-daughter team in the Los Angeles area are staying in their home behind the fire barricades and taking inventory of destroyed properties.
item.dc:creator: Gene Johnson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Los Angeles tree advocates hope their vital work recovers from devastating fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Some people across Los Angeles have worked for years to increase the number of trees that give respite from heat and air pollution. The tree advocates have confronted increasing drought, bad trimming and objections from neighbors who resent leaves and sap.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER and DORANY PINEDA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass 'not worried' about supposed 'animosity' between Trump and California
item.description: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass, as well as other local officials, said they were confident that President-elect Trump would visit the region.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hurricane Katrina victim forced to evacuate again, this time for Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: The California wildfires are hitting too close to home for a woman who used to live in New Orleans. She was forced to evacuate during Hurricane Katrina, and now she's evacuating once again.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Lopez, Fox 5

item.title: New England Patriots hire former Super Bowl champ Mike Vrabel as next coach
item.description: Vrabel, a fan favorite during eight seasons as a player in New England where he was a member of its first three Super Bowl winners and an inductee into the team’s Hall of Fame in 2023, was the preferred candidate because of his long association with the franchise and coaching success.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 24 dead as fire crews try to corral Los Angeles blazes before winds return this week
item.description: Firefighters scrambled to make further progress against wildfires that have killed at least 24 people in the Los Angeles area as forecasters again warned of dangerous weather with the return of strong winds this week.
item.dc:creator: Jae Hong, Holly Ramer And Michael R. Blood, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Los Angeles County medical examiner's office confirms wildfire death toll rises to 16
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles County medical examiner’s office confirmed the death toll from the wildfires ravaging the area has risen to 16. The total of confirmed fatalities stands at 16 victims, and the cases remain under investigation.
item.dc:creator: JAE HONG, HOLLY RAMER and MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Texans get 2 points on blocked extra point return vs. Chargers, 1st in NFL playoff history
item.description: Houston's D'Angelo Ross returned a blocked extra point for two points in the fourth quarter of the Texans' wild-card playoff victory over the Los Angeles Chargers on Saturday — the first such play in NFL postseason history.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Gun jams as woman tries to shoot boyfriend: Tennessee police
item.description: A Tennessee woman is facing an attempted murder charge after reportedly attempting to shoot her boyfriend before her gun jammed, Memphis Police said.
item.dc:creator: Raven Moore, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Residents who fled from California wildfire detail escape
item.description: CHICAGO — Nearly 200,000 people have evacuated their homes in Los Angeles as deadly wildfires continue to rage across the city. The devastating blazes have claimed the lives of at least 10 and destroyed several neighborhoods. WGN-TV spoke with two people from L.A.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Barnes, WGN 9

item.title: Biden takes unexpected, impromptu questions from press in final days
item.description: President Biden opted Friday to voluntarily take a host of questions from the press after delivering remarks on the day’s job report, a speech that had been put on the White House schedule last minute.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Newsom calls for investigation into water supply after tanks 'went dry' during wildfire
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom has ordered an investigation into the Los Angeles Department of Water and Power amid reports of a loss of water pressure to fire hydrants and limited water resources in the wildfire zones.
item.dc:creator: Eytan Wallace, WGN 9

item.title: WATCH: Flying block of snow smashes through Kansas truck driver's windshield
item.description: Clean the snow off your vehicle. It's a warning many northerners have heard before; but one video taken during a recent winter storm illustrates just how dangerous leftover snow can be.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rare gold coin found in red kettle donations for The Salvation Army
item.description: A rare South African Krugerrand gold coin worth thousands was found amid the spare change donated to a Salvation Army red kettle in Colorado.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, KRON4

item.title: Wildfires latest: 153,000 residents still under evacuation as Kenneth Fire order lifted
item.description: Firefighters are hoping for a break Friday from the fierce winds that have fueled massive blazes in the Los Angeles area, killing 10 people, obliterating whole neighborhoods and setting the nation’s second-largest city on edge.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Photos: Smoke, smoldering homes and flames as wildfires ravage Los Angeles area
item.description: All around Los Angeles, scenes of flames were everywhere, as many areas continued to burn all but unabated.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Civilian drone hits, damages firefighting aircraft over Palisades Fire
item.description: A firefighting aircraft that was operating over the Palisades Fire is grounded and out of service after it was struck and damaged by a civilian’s drone on Thursday afternoon, officials confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KRON4

item.title: Actor Steve Guttenberg returns to his wildfire-charred LA neighborhood
item.description: Steve Guttenberg awoke Thursday morning to a grim reality: The treacherous wildfire that tore through the Pacific Palisades had left his once-lush neighborhood charred and unrecognizable.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: LA area's 2 biggest blazes burn at least 10,000 structures, while new fire leads to more evacuations
item.description: The two biggest wildfires ravaging the Los Angeles area have killed at least 10 people and burned more than 10,000 homes and other structures, officials said as they urged more people to heed evacuation orders after a new blaze ignited and quickly grew.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, JULIE WATSON and JOHN SEEWER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Google Trends: Nearly every US state is searching the Broncos over the Bills
item.description: We looked at Google Trends statistics to see whether the Broncos or the Bills were the talk of the country – and one team won by a landslide.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, Fox 5

item.title: New LA-area fire prompts more evacuations while over 10,000 structures lost to the 2 biggest blazes
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The two biggest wildfires ravaging the Los Angeles area have burned at least 10,000 homes, buildings and other structures, officials said Thursday as they urged more people to heed evacuation orders after a new blaze ignited and quickly grew.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, JULIE WATSON and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A sharply divided Supreme Court on Thursday rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s final bid to put his New York hush-money case on hold, clearing the way for him to be sentenced for felony crimes days before he returns to the presidency. The court’s 5-4 order allows Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, MICHAEL HILL and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A powerful winter storm's heavy snow and sleet cancels flights and closes schools across US South
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — A powerful winter storm brought heavy snow and icy conditions to parts of the U.S. South on Thursday, forcing officials to close schools, cancel flights and warn residents in some of the worst-hit areas to stay off roads as it lumbered eastward through Oklahoma and Texas. Arkansas Gov.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE and KENDRIA LAFLEUR, Associated Press, ABC 4
